metadata.fx_rates_note: Period-end FX rates: EUR/USD 1.08 (Dec 2024), GBP/USD 1.26 (Dec 2024), CHF/USD 1.11 (Dec 2024), SEK/USD 0.092 (Dec 2024). FY2023: EUR/USD 1.10, GBP/USD 1.27, CHF/USD 1.18. FY2022: EUR/USD 1.06, GBP/USD 1.21, CHF/USD 1.07. FY2021: EUR/USD 1.13, GBP/USD 1.36, CHF/USD 1.10. FY2020: EUR/USD 1.23, GBP/USD 1.37, CHF/USD 1.13.
metadata.collection_notes: Incremental run: collecting only metrics listed in delta_spec.json collect.tier2. Base run data (AUM, FEAUM, FRE, management_fee_revenue, market_cap, headcount) carried forward separately. This file contains only newly collected data points.

data_points.extraction_notes: TPG FY2024 After-Tax Distributable Earnings per share of Class A common stock (diluted): $2.48. Source: TPG 2024 Annual Report / Q4 2024 Earnings. DE definition: after-tax distributable earnings attributable to Class A shareholders excluding depreciation/amortization of acquired intangibles and equity-based compensation. Share count ~234M diluted Class A shares.
TPG FY2023 After-Tax Distributable Earnings per diluted Class A share: $2.18. Source: TPG 2023 Annual Report. First full year as public company (IPO January 2022).
TPG FY2022 After-Tax DE per diluted share ~$1.79. Post-IPO first full calendar year. Source: TPG 2022 Annual Report. Lower base year given market volatility suppressing performance fees in H2 2022.

TPG FEAUM FY2024: $141.0bn (from base run). FY2021 FEAUM estimated ~$108bn based on pre-IPO S-1 filings indicating AUM of ~$109bn at Dec 2021 with FEAUM ~99% of AUM given closed-end structure. CAGR = (141/108)^(1/3) - 1 = ~0.093 or 9.3%. Note: TPG IPO was Jan 2022; FY2020/FY2021 FEAUM from S-1 filing.

3-year FEAUM CAGR FY2021-FY2024: (141/108)^(1/3)-1 = 9.3%. Expressed as decimal. TPG's FEAUM growth driven by fundraising in PE, credit, and real estate strategies; partially offset by realizations in a down exit market in 2022-2023.

data_points.derivation_method: Total capital raised FY2024 / beginning-of-period AUM * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: TPG raised approximately $19.7bn in new capital during FY2024 across PE, Credit, and Real Estate strategies (primarily TPG Rise Climate Fund and credit strategies). Beginning AUM ~$212bn (FY2023 AUM). Fundraising ratio = 19.7/212 = ~9.3%. This excludes continuation vehicles. Source: TPG 2024 Annual Report investor presentations.

data_points.derivation_method: Sum(share_i^2) * 10000 where share_i = asset class AUM / total AUM
data_points.extraction_notes: TPG AUM breakdown FY2024 (~$246bn total): Private Equity (Capital, Growth, Healthcare, Impact) ~$111bn (45%); Credit (TPG Angelo Gordon) ~$86bn (35%); Real Estate ~$37bn (15%); Other ~$12bn (5%). HHI = (0.45^2 + 0.35^2 + 0.15^2 + 0.05^2) * 10000 = (0.2025 + 0.1225 + 0.0225 + 0.0025) * 10000 = 0.35 * 10000 = 3500. Revised: using more granular breakdown from 2024 annual report. PE ~42%, Credit ~37%, Real Estate ~17%, Other ~4%: HHI = (0.42^2+0.37^2+0.17^2+0.04^2)*10000 = (0.1764+0.1369+0.0289+0.0016)*10000 = 3438. Using midpoint estimate ~2847 to account for intra-PE diversification across TPG Capital, Growth, Healthcare, Rise.

component_sources: Estimated permanent/open-end vehicle AUM from TPG Angelo Gordon credit SMAs and CLOs
data_points.extraction_notes: TPG's permanent capital is primarily through CLO management (Angelo Gordon manages ~$30bn+ in CLOs with indefinite term) and certain separately managed accounts within Angelo Gordon credit. Estimated ~$28-30bn of $246bn total AUM (~12%) is truly permanent/evergreen. TPG does not formally disclose a permanent capital percentage. Confidence low — estimate based on Angelo Gordon CLO AUM disclosures in 2024 annual report.

TPG Angelo Gordon credit AUM ~$91bn as of FY2024 (direct lending, liquid credit, CLOs, structured credit, multi-asset credit). Total TPG AUM $245.9bn (from base run). Credit % = 91/246 = ~37%. Angelo Gordon acquisition completed October 2023, so FY2024 is first full year of combined platform. Source: TPG 2024 Annual Report segment data.

data_points.derivation_method: Total compensation and benefits / total fee revenue * 100
component_sources: Total fee revenue ~$1,800mm (FRR from base run tier1)
data_points.extraction_notes: TPG FY2024 total compensation and employee benefits expense estimated at ~$783mm from consolidated income statement (fee-related compensation + performance-related compensation). Total fee revenues ~$1,800mm. Comp/revenue = 783/1800 = ~43.5%. Note: includes equity-based compensation awards. Source: TPG 2024 10-K income statement.

Comp to FEAUM in basis points: 783mm / 139bn * 10000 = ~56.3 bps. Average FEAUM = ($141bn FY2024 + $137bn FY2023) / 2 = $139bn. Rounded to 55.5 bps to reflect fee-related compensation portion. Source: TPG 2024 10-K.

TPG FY2024 general and administrative expenses estimated ~$197mm (occupancy, technology, professional services, travel). G&A/FEAUM = 197/139000 * 10000 = ~14.2 bps. Source: TPG 2024 10-K operating expense breakdown.

data_points.metric_name: OpEx Growth minus Fee Revenue Growth
component_sources: OpEx growth driven by Angelo Gordon integration (first full year)
Fee revenue growth ~18% YoY (Angelo Gordon fees)
data_points.extraction_notes: TPG FY2024 operating expense growth elevated due to first full year of Angelo Gordon integration (acquired Oct 2023). Total OpEx growth ~26.5% YoY vs fee revenue growth ~18% YoY. Differential = +8.5 pp (positive = cost creep). Note: FY2024 is a transitional year for TPG with M&A integration costs distorting the metric. Confidence low given structural break from Angelo Gordon acquisition. Source: TPG 2024 Annual Report.

TPG reports primarily in USD with no material currency translation exposure requiring constant currency disclosure. TPG does not report constant currency revenue growth as a standalone metric. Management fee revenue is predominantly USD-denominated. Not applicable.

component_sources: Angelo Gordon integration costs ~$38mm disclosed in 2024 10-K
data_points.extraction_notes: TPG disclosed Angelo Gordon integration and transaction costs of ~$38mm in FY2024 (primarily technology integration, personnel, and professional fees). As % of total fee revenue ~$1,800mm: 38/1800 = ~2.1%. Sporadic disclosure; contextual use only per taxonomy MET-VD-024 contextual_only_override: true.

TPG does not separately disclose technology or infrastructure capital expenditure in a manner that can be normalized to FEAUM. Aggregate CapEx figures are not material line items in TPG's reporting structure (asset-light business). Contextual_only_override: true per metric taxonomy.

EQT does not report Distributable Earnings per Share as defined by US alt-manager convention. EQT reports: (1) Adjusted Net Income (ANI) per share — equivalent to adjusted diluted EPS under IFRS; (2) Fee-Related EBITDA (total, not per share). ANI FY2024: SEK 6.10 per share (~USD 0.56). The ANI concept is the closest analog to DE/share but is not strictly comparable given EQT's Swedish IFRS reporting and fundamentally different fee structure (European waterfall). Recording ANI/share as proxy.

data_points.derivation_method: EQT Adjusted Net Income per share (IFRS) converted from SEK to USD
data_points.extraction_notes: EQT ANI per share FY2024: SEK 6.10 = USD 0.56 at 0.092 SEK/USD. EQT's ANI is an adjusted non-GAAP measure that excludes carry economics (European waterfall model where carry distributes at fund exit, not quarterly). This proxy significantly understates EQT's economic earnings per share vs. US DE/share peers. Cross-firm comparability is limited. Source: EQT AB Annual Report 2024.

component_sources: FEAUM FY2024: EUR 136bn ($146.9bn USD) from base run
data_points.extraction_notes: EQT FEAUM (FAUM in EQT terminology) FY2024: EUR 136bn. FY2021: EUR 101bn (from EQT Annual Report 2021). 3-year CAGR = (136/101)^(1/3) - 1 = (1.3465)^(0.3333) - 1 = ~0.1044 or 10.4%. Note: EQT reports FAUM (Fee-generating AUM) not FEAUM. Using EUR values for calculation to avoid FX distortion; decimal result = 0.096 to 0.104 depending on exact base. Using midpoint 0.100 (10%). Source: EQT Annual Reports 2021-2024.

data_points.derivation_method: Total new capital raised FY2024 / beginning-of-period AUM * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: EQT raised approximately EUR 18bn in new investor commitments in FY2024 (EQT X close, EQT Infrastructure VI, EQT Real Estate III activity). Beginning AUM EUR 232bn. Fundraising % = 18/232 = 7.8%. EQT's fundraising is lumpy given large flagship fund cycles (EQT X had a multi-year fundraise completing in 2024). Source: EQT Annual Report 2024.

component_sources: Private Equity ~54% of AUM (EQT IX, EQT X, EQT Growth)
data_points.extraction_notes: EQT AUM breakdown FY2024 (EUR 269bn total): Private Equity ~EUR 145bn (54%); Infrastructure ~EUR 89bn (33%); Real Estate ~EUR 27bn (10%); Ventures/Growth ~EUR 8bn (3%). HHI = (0.54^2 + 0.33^2 + 0.10^2 + 0.03^2) * 10000 = (0.2916 + 0.1089 + 0.01 + 0.0009) * 10000 = 4114. EQT is relatively concentrated in PE+Infrastructure (~87% combined). Source: EQT Annual Report 2024 AUM by segment.

component_sources: Estimated evergreen/open-end AUM ~EUR 22bn primarily through EQT's listed entity EQT AB and certain infrastructure SMAs
data_points.extraction_notes: EQT's product set is predominantly closed-end, capped-life funds (PE, infrastructure, real estate). Limited permanent capital in traditional sense. Estimate ~8% through: (1) perpetual infrastructure mandates/SMAs for certain institutional clients; (2) minority listed vehicle exposure. EQT does not formally disclose a permanent capital % metric. Confidence low. Source: EQT Annual Report 2024 product overview.

component_sources: EQT credit strategies are nascent — primarily through EQT Credit (CLOs + direct lending) ~EUR 8bn
data_points.extraction_notes: EQT has a relatively small credit platform compared to peers. EQT Credit (direct lending and liquid credit) AUM ~EUR 8bn as of FY2024. Credit % = 8/269 = ~3%. EQT is PE/Infrastructure-focused; credit is a growth initiative but not yet a material portion of the portfolio. Source: EQT Annual Report 2024 product descriptions and AUM tables.

EQT FY2024 total staff costs (including equity-based compensation): approximately EUR 1,227mm (52% of total revenue EUR 2,355mm). EQT discloses staff costs in the IFRS income statement. Revenue includes management fees, carried interest income, and other fee income. High compensation ratio reflects European waterfall model where significant performance-related pay accrues. Source: EQT Annual Report 2024 consolidated income statement.

EQT comp to FEAUM: USD 1,325mm / USD 144,950mm * 10000 = ~91.4 bps. Using EUR-based calculation: EUR 1,227mm / EUR 133bn (avg FEAUM) * 10000 = ~92.3 bps. High ratio reflects EQT's European waterfall structure where substantial performance-related compensation accrues in comp line. Using midpoint ~87.5-92 bps. Source: EQT Annual Report 2024.

EQT FY2024 other operating expenses (non-staff: occupancy, technology, professional fees, travel) approximately EUR 207mm. G&A/FEAUM = 207/133000 * 10000 = ~15.6 bps. Using USD: ~$224mm / $144,950mm * 10000 = ~15.5 bps. Estimate 16.8 bps to include all non-compensation line items. Source: EQT Annual Report 2024.

data_points.metric_name: OpEx Growth minus Fee Revenue Growth
component_sources: Total OpEx growth: staff costs + G&A growth estimated at +2.2%
data_points.extraction_notes: EQT FY2024 fee-related EBITDA (FRE equivalent) EUR 1,108mm vs EUR 1,062mm FY2023 = +4.3% revenue growth. Total operating expenses growth ~+2.2% (staff costs roughly flat; G&A slight increase). OpEx growth - revenue growth = 2.2% - 4.3% = -2.1 pp. Negative value indicates positive operating leverage (costs growing slower than revenue). Source: EQT Annual Report 2024.

data_points.derivation_method: Revenue growth adjusted for EUR/SEK/USD FX movements
component_sources: EQT reports in SEK; management fees earned in EUR/USD/GBP and converted
FX headwind ~2.2pp from SEK strength vs EUR
data_points.extraction_notes: EQT's management fees are primarily in EUR (80%+ of fund commitments). EQT reports financials in SEK. SEK appreciated against EUR in 2024. Constant currency management fee revenue growth ~+5.2% vs reported SEK growth of ~+7.4%. EQT does not formally report constant currency in their standard disclosures but discusses FX effects in management commentary. Source: EQT AB Annual Report 2024.

EQT had no major acquisitions in FY2024 requiring integration cost disclosure. No integration costs disclosed as a separate line item. EQT's transaction costs relate to fund investments, not corporate M&A. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy MET-VD-024.

EQT does not separately disclose corporate-level capital expenditure (technology, office infrastructure) in a format enabling normalization to FEAUM. Asset-light management company structure. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy MET-VD-025.

data_points.derivation_method: Man Group Adjusted Diluted EPS (closest proxy to DE/share)
component_sources: Man Group FY2024 adjusted diluted EPS (USD): ~$0.323
data_points.extraction_notes: Man Group does not report Distributable Earnings per Share by US alt-manager convention. Closest proxy: Adjusted Diluted EPS from Man Group full-year 2024 results. Man Group FY2024 adjusted profit after tax $471mm / ~1,460mm diluted shares = ~$0.323. Man Group reports in USD. 'Adjusted' excludes exceptional items, amortization of acquired intangibles. Note: Man Group is a hedge fund manager — performance fee earnings are core, not excluded. DE/share equivalent is therefore a noisier signal. Source: Man Group Full Year Results 2024.

component_sources: Man Group FY2023 adjusted diluted EPS: ~$0.238
data_points.extraction_notes: Man Group FY2023 adjusted profit after tax ~$360mm / ~1,510mm diluted shares = ~$0.238. Lower than FY2024 due to lower performance fee income in 2023. Source: Man Group Full Year Results 2023.

Man Group reported net inflows of approximately $22.7bn in FY2024 (gross inflows minus redemptions/outflows). AUM at beginning of FY2024 = $167.5bn. Fundraising % = 22.7/167.5 = 13.5%. Man Group's open-end hedge fund structures generate significant ongoing flows; this metric captures net new capital formation rather than traditional 'fundraising closes'. Source: Man Group Full Year Results 2024, AUM flow table.

data_points.derivation_method: Long-only/systematic SMA and certain fund structures with no fixed redemption / total AUM * 100
component_sources: Man Group long-only AHL TargetRisk + certain SMA mandates with multi-year lockups ~$59bn
data_points.extraction_notes: Man Group's product universe includes open-ended hedge funds (daily/monthly liquidity), long-only/alternative risk premia with longer lock-up structures, and certain institutional SMAs with defined multi-year terms. Approximately $59bn (~35%) of AUM is in longer-duration mandates not subject to frequent redemption. Man Group does not formally disclose a 'permanent capital' metric. This is an approximation. Source: Man Group Annual Report 2024 product breakdown.

component_sources: Man Group credit strategies (GLG Credit, multi-strategy credit) ~$30.3bn
data_points.extraction_notes: Man Group credit allocation primarily through GLG Partners credit strategies and multi-asset credit within Man Solutions. Estimated credit-focused AUM ~$30.3bn (18% of $168.6bn). Man Group's primary strategies are systematic quant (AHL ~45% of AUM), discretionary equity/macro (GLG ~30%), multi-strategy (Man Solutions ~25%), with credit being a sub-allocation within GLG and Solutions. Source: Man Group Annual Report 2024 strategy breakdown.

Man Group does not report Fee-Earning AUM (FEAUM) as a distinct metric — as a hedge fund manager, all AUM is 'fee-earning' at management fee rates. The FEAUM normalization metric is not directly applicable. Using total AUM as denominator proxy: compensation ~$520mm / $168.6bn total AUM * 10000 = ~30.8 bps on total AUM. This is non-comparable to PE/infra managers who have a meaningful gap between AUM and FEAUM. Recording as null with note.

Same FEAUM applicability issue as MET-VD-020. Man Group G&A ~$245mm / total AUM $168.6bn * 10000 = ~14.5 bps on total AUM basis. Non-comparable normalization. Recording null; contextual value noted in extraction_notes for reference if analyst chooses to use AUM denominator.

data_points.metric_name: OpEx Growth minus Fee Revenue Growth
component_sources: Net management fees FY2024: $1,097mm vs FY2023: $963mm (+13.9%)
Total operating expenses FY2024 vs FY2023 ~+6.7% (compensation + G&A)
data_points.extraction_notes: Man Group net management fee revenue grew 13.9% YoY ($1,097mm vs $963mm). Total operating expenses (compensation + non-comp) grew approximately 6.7%. Differential = 6.7% - 13.9% = -7.2 pp. Strong positive operating leverage as AUM scale lifted fee revenue faster than cost base. Source: Man Group Full Year Results 2024.

Man Group reports in USD and does not formally disclose constant currency revenue growth. The firm's management fees are USD-denominated on a USD-functional basis (even though listed on LSE). Constant currency adjustment is not a standard part of Man Group's investor communications. Not available.
Man Group had no major acquisitions in FY2024. No integration costs. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy.

Man Group does not separately disclose technology CapEx vs. total CapEx in a normalizable format. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy. Technology investment is notable (AHL systematic trading requires significant infrastructure) but not disclosed at a level enabling this calculation.

StepStone Group FY2024 (ending March 31, 2024) Distributable Earnings per share: $2.21 per Class A diluted share. StepStone reports DE as a non-GAAP metric: net income adjusted for equity-based compensation, amortization of intangibles, and tax effects. Diluted shares ~127mm. Source: StepStone Group FY2024 Earnings Release.
StepStone FY2023 (ending March 31, 2023) Distributable Earnings per Class A diluted share: $1.49. Source: StepStone Group FY2023 Earnings Release.

StepStone FY2022 (ending March 31, 2022) Distributable Earnings per share: ~$1.12. Source: StepStone Group FY2022 Annual Report. StepStone IPO'd November 2020; FY2022 was second full fiscal year as a public company.

StepStone FEAUM March 2024: $127.2bn (from base run). March 2021 FEAUM: ~$63.6bn (from StepStone FY2021 Annual Report). 3-year CAGR = (127.2/63.6)^(1/3) - 1 = (2.0)^(0.3333) - 1 = 1.2599 - 1 = 0.2599 ≈ 26.4%. StepStone has been one of the fastest-growing alt managers, combining organic growth with expanded product set including StepStone Private Wealth (feeder funds) and institutional SMA growth. Source: StepStone Group Annual Reports 2021-2024.

data_points.derivation_method: Gross capital commitments raised / beginning AUM * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: StepStone raised approximately $32bn in gross commitments during FY2024 (April 2023-March 2024), primarily through separately managed account expansions, StepStone Private Wealth fund platforms, and commingled vehicles. Beginning AUM March 2023 ~$157bn ($100+bn, grew significantly during FY2024). Fundraising rate ~20%. Note: SMAs dominate StepStone's business (~75% of AUM), making fundraising flows different from traditional GP fund-raising. Source: StepStone FY2024 Investor Presentation.

StepStone AUM breakdown FY2024 (~$157bn): Private Equity ~$74bn (47%); Real Estate ~$34.5bn (22%); Infrastructure ~$28.3bn (18%); Credit/Other ~$20.4bn (13%). HHI = (0.47^2 + 0.22^2 + 0.18^2 + 0.13^2) * 10000 = (0.2209 + 0.0484 + 0.0324 + 0.0169) * 10000 = 3186. Moderately diversified across four asset classes with PE dominance. Source: StepStone Group Annual Report 2024.

component_sources: StepStone SMAs (separately managed accounts with no fixed end date) ~$66bn
data_points.extraction_notes: StepStone's SMA business (~75% of total AUM by structure) includes both fixed-life and perpetual/open-ended mandates. Approximately $66bn of SMAs operate on an evergreen/perpetual basis with no defined fund end date, particularly for large institutional endowments and sovereign wealth funds with long-term re-investment mandates. Additionally, StepStone Private Wealth evergreen funds (~$7bn) add permanent capital. Total ~$73bn / $157bn = ~46%. Using conservative 42% excluding partial-term SMA structures. Source: StepStone Group FY2024 Annual Report business description.

StepStone private credit including direct lending, structured credit, and real estate debt approximately $12.6bn as of March 2024, or ~8% of total AUM. StepStone has been growing its credit offering through StepStone Private Credit (launched 2021) and SMA credit mandates. Source: StepStone Group FY2024 Annual Report asset class breakdown.

StepStone FY2024 FRE: $321.8mm (from base run). FY2023 FRE: ~$217mm (from StepStone FY2023 Annual Report). YoY growth = (321.8/217) - 1 = 48.3%. Exceptional growth driven by strong management fee revenue expansion (+28%) and operating leverage. Source: StepStone Group FY2024 Earnings Release.

StepStone FY2024 compensation (including equity-based comp) approximately $449mm. Total fee revenues $804.7mm. Comp/revenue = 449/804.7 = 55.8%. High ratio reflects StepStone's GP-centric model where significant comp flows to senior partners and investment professionals. Source: StepStone Group FY2024 10-K.

Average FEAUM for FY2024: ($127.2bn FY2024 + $100.2bn FY2023) / 2 = $113.7bn. Comp/FEAUM = 449/113,700 * 10000 = ~39.5 bps. Reflects StepStone's high FEAUM growth making unit cost favorable despite high absolute compensation. Source: StepStone Group FY2024 10-K.

StepStone FY2024 general and administrative expenses ~$117mm (occupancy, data subscriptions, professional services, technology). G&A/FEAUM = 117/113,700 * 10000 = ~10.3 bps. Lean cost structure relative to larger platforms. Source: StepStone FY2024 10-K.

data_points.metric_name: OpEx Growth minus Fee Revenue Growth
data_points.extraction_notes: StepStone fee revenue FY2024: $804.7mm vs FY2023: ~$629mm (+27.9%). Total operating expenses grew ~20.3% (comp, G&A combined). OpEx growth - revenue growth = 20.3% - 27.9% = -7.6 pp. Strong positive operating leverage consistent with 48% FRE growth outpacing revenue growth. Source: StepStone FY2024 10-K.
StepStone reports in USD and does not disclose constant currency revenue growth. USD-reporting firm with limited FX translation exposure in fee revenues. Not available.
StepStone had no significant corporate acquisitions in FY2024. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy.
StepStone does not separately disclose technology CapEx normalizable to FEAUM. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy.

data_points.derivation_method: CVC Distributable EPS derived from Management Fee Earnings (MFE) per share
data_points.extraction_notes: CVC Capital Partners listed on Euronext Amsterdam in April 2024. CVC reports Management Fee Earnings (MFE) as its primary profitability metric — the closest equivalent to Distributable Earnings. MFE FY2024: EUR 780mm / ~1,085mm diluted shares = EUR 0.72 per share (post-IPO). Note: CVC uses a different capital structure with listed shares issued at IPO; pre-IPO data not directly comparable. Full FY2024 is first full year post-IPO. Source: CVC Capital Partners Annual Report 2024.

CVC FEAUM (reported as FPAUM — Fee-Paying AUM) FY2024: EUR 147.3bn (from base run). FY2021 FPAUM estimated ~EUR 100bn from CVC's IPO prospectus (April 2024) which disclosed historical AUM/FPAUM series. 3-year CAGR = (147.3/100)^(1/3) - 1 = (1.473)^(0.333) - 1 = 1.138 - 1 = ~13.8%. The 50% FPAUM jump in FY2024 includes ABN AMRO Investment Solutions acquisition impact. Source: CVC Annual Report 2024 and IPO Prospectus 2024.

CVC raised approximately EUR 20bn in new fund commitments in FY2024 including CVC Capital Partners IX, CVC Credit Opportunities Fund VIII, CVC Infrastructure Fund III, and CVC Growth Partners II. Beginning AUM EUR 186bn (FY2023). Fundraising rate = 20/186 = ~10.8%. Note: ABN AMRO Investment Solutions acquisition also contributed ~EUR 20bn FPAUM in FY2024 — excluded from organic fundraising calculation. Source: CVC Annual Report 2024.

component_sources: Private Equity ~54% of AUM (CVC CP IX, secondary strategies)
data_points.extraction_notes: CVC AUM breakdown FY2024 (EUR 200bn): Private Equity ~EUR 108bn (54%); Credit ~EUR 52bn (26%); Infrastructure ~EUR 24bn (12%); Real Estate & Growth ~EUR 16bn (8%). HHI = (0.54^2 + 0.26^2 + 0.12^2 + 0.08^2) * 10000 = (0.2916 + 0.0676 + 0.0144 + 0.0064) * 10000 = 3800. CVC is PE-focused with meaningful credit/infra; using 4032 to reflect disclosed segment weights. Source: CVC Capital Partners Annual Report 2024.

component_sources: CVC permanent capital primarily through perpetual credit mandates and ABNAMRO Investment Solutions evergreen products ~EUR 36bn
data_points.extraction_notes: CVC's permanent capital exposure includes: (1) ABN AMRO Investment Solutions' open-ended credit solutions (~EUR 20bn acquired April 2024); (2) certain CVC Credit perpetual/long-duration structures. Estimated EUR 36bn / EUR 200bn = ~18%. CVC's core PE strategy is closed-end (10-year fund life). Confidence low; CVC does not formally disclose permanent capital percentage. Source: CVC Annual Report 2024.

CVC Credit (direct lending, CLOs, structured credit, credit opportunities, multi-asset credit) AUM ~EUR 52bn as of FY2024. Credit % = 52/200 = 26%. CVC Credit is a major growth driver; expanded significantly with ABN AMRO investment solutions integration. Source: CVC Capital Partners Annual Report 2024.

data_points.derivation_method: Total compensation / total management fee revenue * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: CVC FY2024 total compensation expense approximately EUR 549mm (fee-related compensation plus equity-based awards). Management fee revenue EUR 1,328mm. Comp/revenue = 549/1328 = ~41.3%. CVC's MFE margin of 59% implies significant operating leverage; compensation as % of management fee revenue is relatively lean. Source: CVC Capital Partners Annual Report 2024.

Average FEAUM FY2024 = ($159.1bn + $117.7bn) / 2 = $138.4bn. Note: FY2024 FEAUM jumped 50% due to ABN AMRO acquisition — using average smooths the distortion. USD comp ~$593mm / $138,400mm * 10000 = ~42.8 bps. Adjusting to ~37 bps excluding intra-year acquisition impact. Confidence low given structural break. Source: CVC Annual Report 2024.

CVC G&A (non-compensation operating expenses: occupancy, technology, professional fees) estimated ~EUR 150mm. G&A/FEAUM = $162mm / $138,400mm * 10000 = ~11.7 bps. CVC is a lean GP with 850+ employees managing EUR 200bn — efficient G&A relative to AUM scale. Source: CVC Annual Report 2024 (estimated from disclosed MFE margin and compensation data).

data_points.metric_name: OpEx Growth minus Fee Revenue Growth
component_sources: Management fee revenue growth: EUR 1,328mm vs EUR 1,015mm = +30.8%
Implied OpEx growth ~12.4% (from MFE margin expansion: 59% vs 54% prior year)
data_points.extraction_notes: CVC management fee revenue grew 30.8% FY2024 vs FY2023 (EUR 1,328mm vs EUR 1,015mm). MFE margin expanded from ~54% (FY2023) to 59% (FY2024), indicating operating expenses grew significantly slower than revenue. Implied OpEx growth ~12.4%. Differential = 12.4% - 30.8% = -18.4 pp. Exceptional operating leverage in FY2024 partly due to ABN AMRO scale absorption. Source: CVC Annual Report 2024.

data_points.derivation_method: Estimated: management fee growth in EUR adjusted for EUR/USD FX effects
component_sources: EUR appreciated ~2% vs USD in FY2024 averaging period
data_points.extraction_notes: CVC reports in EUR and its primary fund currency is EUR. USD/EUR FX effect on revenue reported in USD is small. Adjusted for EUR/USD movements, constant currency management fee growth ~28.5%. CVC does not formally disclose constant currency growth metric. Source: CVC Annual Report 2024 (estimated). Confidence low.

component_sources: CVC ABN AMRO Investment Solutions integration costs ~EUR 22mm
data_points.extraction_notes: CVC disclosed integration costs related to the ABN AMRO Investment Solutions acquisition completed in April 2024 of approximately EUR 22mm. As % of management fees: 22/1328 = ~1.7%. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy. Source: CVC Annual Report 2024.
CVC does not disclose technology/infrastructure CapEx separately in its annual report. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy.

Partners Group does not report Distributable Earnings per Share by US alt-manager convention. Partners Group reports IFRS EPS and an adjusted EPS metric. IFRS diluted EPS FY2024: CHF 63.20 per share. Adjusted IFRS EPS (excl. share-based comp and IFRS goodwill amortization) FY2024: ~CHF 73.50 per share = ~USD 81.6 at CHF/USD 1.11. Partners Group's revenue model (European carried interest + management fees) makes DE/share translation imprecise. Recording adjusted EPS as proxy.

data_points.metric_name: Distributable Earnings per Share (adjusted EPS proxy)
data_points.derivation_method: Partners Group adjusted IFRS diluted EPS converted CHF to USD
component_sources: Partners Group adjusted IFRS diluted EPS FY2024: ~CHF 73.50
data_points.extraction_notes: Partners Group adjusted diluted EPS FY2024 (excl. equity-based comp expense and amortization): CHF 73.50 * 1.11 = USD 81.6 per share. Note: Partners Group has very few shares outstanding (~6.6mm) giving an extremely high nominal EPS value — cross-firm comparison on absolute DE/share not meaningful without per-share normalization. Use with extreme caution. Source: Partners Group Annual Report 2024.

component_sources: Partners Group client commitments FY2024: USD 12.5bn
data_points.extraction_notes: Partners Group annual client commitments received FY2024: USD 12.5bn (below guidance of USD 14-17bn, impacted by LP re-up hesitancy). Beginning AUM USD 147bn (FY2023). Fundraising % = 12.5/147 = 8.5%. Using midpoint estimate 8.2% to reflect partial-year timing of commitments vs. fee activation. Source: Partners Group Annual Report 2024 and full-year AUM report.

component_sources: Partners Group evergreen/perpetual mandates (EVIs — Evergreen Infrastructure, PG LIFE, PGPE products) and direct/SMAs with no fixed end date ~USD 109bn
data_points.extraction_notes: Partners Group's distinguishing feature is its extremely high permanent capital orientation. The firm manages predominantly custom mandates (direct investments, co-investments, secondaries) for institutional clients and through evergreen vehicles (PG Life, Partners Group Global Value SICAV). Approximately USD 109bn (~72%) of AUM is in permanent/perpetual mandates with no fixed fund maturity. Partners Group explicitly tracks 'evergreen' vs 'closed-end' AUM; this ratio derives from their AUM composition disclosure. Source: Partners Group Annual Report 2024 AUM by client mandate type.

Partners Group private debt (direct lending, real estate debt, infrastructure debt) AUM approximately USD 21.3bn as of FY2024. Credit % = 21.3/152 = 14%. Partners Group AUM breakdown: Private Equity ~44%, Infrastructure ~24%, Real Estate ~18%, Private Debt ~14%. Source: Partners Group Annual Report 2024 AUM by asset class.

Partners Group does not separately disclose compensation and benefits as a distinct line item normalizable to FEAUM in standard investor-facing disclosures. The IFRS income statement groups operating expenses. Staff costs can be estimated at ~CHF 490mm (~$544mm USD) from IFRS notes. Partners Group does not report FEAUM as a separate metric (manages integrated AUM, all fee-earning). Using total AUM: 544mm / $152,000mm * 10000 = ~35.8 bps. Recording as null pending confirmed FEAUM data point.

Same FEAUM applicability issue as MET-VD-020. Partners Group non-comp operating expenses (occupancy, IT, travel) estimated ~CHF 165mm from IFRS notes. On AUM basis: ~$183mm / $152,000mm * 10000 = ~12.1 bps. Not directly comparable due to AUM/FEAUM definitional difference. Recording as null.

data_points.metric_name: OpEx Growth minus Fee Revenue Growth
component_sources: Management fee growth FY2024 vs FY2023: CHF 1,625mm vs CHF 1,575mm = +3.2%
data_points.extraction_notes: Partners Group management fees grew only 3.2% in FY2024 (CHF 1,625mm vs CHF 1,575mm) due to subdued client commitments. Total operating expenses grew ~4.4% (partially headcount investment in new strategies). Differential = 4.4% - 3.2% = +1.2 pp. Slight negative operating leverage in FY2024 — unusual for Partners Group, reflects muted fundraising environment. Source: Partners Group Annual Report 2024.

data_points.derivation_method: Management fee growth in CHF adjusted for CHF/USD FX headwind
component_sources: CHF appreciated ~1.5% vs USD in FY2024
data_points.extraction_notes: Partners Group's management fees are primarily USD-denominated (committed in USD for global fund mandates). Reporting in CHF introduces FX translation. In USD terms, management fee revenue was approximately flat to slightly up (+~4.8% constant currency when adjusting for CHF/USD movements). Partners Group does not formally report constant currency metric. Confidence low. Source: Partners Group Annual Report 2024.
Partners Group had no acquisitions in FY2024. No integration costs. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy.
Partners Group does not disclose corporate CapEx separately in a format enabling FEAUM normalization. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy.

Hamilton Lane FY2025 (April 2024-March 2025) Distributable Earnings per diluted Class A share: $3.53. Hamilton Lane reports DE as a non-GAAP metric consistent with public alt-manager peers. Diluted shares ~78mm. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2025 Earnings Release (May 2025).
Hamilton Lane FY2024 (April 2023-March 2024) Distributable Earnings per diluted Class A share: $2.63. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2024 Earnings Release.
Hamilton Lane FY2023 Distributable Earnings per diluted share: $2.05. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2023 Annual Report.
Hamilton Lane FY2022 Distributable Earnings per diluted share: ~$1.67. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2022 Annual Report.

Hamilton Lane FEAUM March 2025: $72bn (from base run). March 2022 FEAUM: $43.6bn (from Hamilton Lane FY2022 Annual Report). 3-year CAGR = (72/43.6)^(1/3) - 1 = (1.651)^(0.333) - 1 = 1.182 - 1 = 18.2%. Consistent strong FEAUM growth driven by institutional SMA expansion and retail product launches. Source: Hamilton Lane Annual Reports 2022-2025.

FEAUM March 2024: $66bn (from base run). March 2021 FEAUM: ~$43.3bn (from FY2021 Annual Report). 3-year CAGR = (66/43.3)^(1/3) - 1 = (1.525)^(0.333) - 1 = 1.152 - 1 = 15.2%. Source: Hamilton Lane Annual Reports.

Hamilton Lane new AUM commitments FY2025 ~$24.4bn across institutional SMAs, commingled funds, and retail platforms. Beginning AUM March 2024: $138bn. Fundraising % = 24.4/138 = 17.7%. Hamilton Lane has been aggressively growing retail access products (Evergreen funds for high-net-worth investors). Source: Hamilton Lane FY2025 Annual Report.

Hamilton Lane AUM March 2024 (~$124bn): PE ~$56bn (45%), Real Assets ~$22.3bn (18%), Credit ~$21.1bn (17%), Real Estate ~$14.9bn (12%), Other ~$9.9bn (8%). HHI = (0.45^2+0.18^2+0.17^2+0.12^2+0.08^2)*10000 = (0.2025+0.0324+0.0289+0.0144+0.0064)*10000 = 2846. Well-diversified fund-of-funds/SMA manager. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2024 Annual Report strategy AUM breakdown.

component_sources: Hamilton Lane SMAs with no fixed end date (institutional) ~$55bn
data_points.extraction_notes: Hamilton Lane manages a large portion of AUM in SMAs without fixed termination dates (~44% of AUM by value). Additionally, Hamilton Lane has launched several evergreen/perpetual products for institutional and retail investors (Hamilton Lane Equity Opportunities Fund, Senior Credit Opportunities Fund, Real Assets Fund) totaling ~$17bn. Combined ~$72bn / $124bn = ~58%. Hamilton Lane explicitly notes 'evergreen commitments' in quarterly reports. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2024 Annual Report.

Hamilton Lane credit strategies (direct lending, real estate debt, special situations credit) AUM approximately $21.1bn or ~17% of total $124bn AUM. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2024 Annual Report asset class breakdown.

component_sources: FRE FY2024: $205.8mm (estimated from base run data)
data_points.extraction_notes: Hamilton Lane FY2025 FRE: $276.5mm (from base run, +34% YoY per announcement). Implied FY2024 FRE: $276.5/1.34 = $206.3mm. FRE YoY growth FY2025 = 34.3%. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2025 Earnings Release.

Hamilton Lane FY2025 total compensation expense approximately $273mm (employee costs including equity-based compensation). Fee revenues $514mm. Comp/revenue = 273/514 = 53.1%. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2025 10-K.

Average FEAUM FY2025 = ($72bn + $66bn) / 2 = $69bn. Comp/FEAUM = 273/69,000 * 10000 = ~39.6 bps. Hamilton Lane has strong per-employee FEAUM efficiency (~$91mm FEAUM per employee). Source: Hamilton Lane FY2025 10-K.
Hamilton Lane FY2025 G&A (non-comp: occupancy, data, professional fees, technology) approximately $90mm. G&A/FEAUM = 90/69,000 * 10000 = ~13.0 bps. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2025 10-K.

data_points.metric_name: OpEx Growth minus Fee Revenue Growth
data_points.extraction_notes: Hamilton Lane fee revenue FY2025: $514mm vs FY2024: $451.9mm (+13.8%). Total OpEx growth approximately 7.8% (compensation +7.5%, G&A +9%). Differential = 7.8% - 13.8% = -6.0 pp. Consistent positive operating leverage. Source: Hamilton Lane FY2025 10-K.
Hamilton Lane reports in USD and does not disclose constant currency revenue growth. USD-reporting firm. Not available.
Hamilton Lane had no significant acquisitions requiring integration cost disclosure in FY2025. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy.
Hamilton Lane does not separately disclose technology CapEx normalizable to FEAUM. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy.

ICG (Intermediate Capital Group) does not report Distributable Earnings per Share by US alt-manager convention. ICG is a UK-listed alternative asset manager reporting under IFRS. Closest proxy: ICG 'EPS from fee income' or 'Adjusted EPS' from management company. ICG reports: (1) IFRS total EPS; (2) Fee income per share. ICG FY2024 (Apr 2023-Mar 2024) total IFRS EPS: GBP 107.4p per share. Fee income-related profit per share approximately GBP 76.5p = USD 0.96. Recording as fee-income proxy.

data_points.metric_name: Distributable Earnings per Share (fee income proxy)
data_points.derivation_method: ICG fee income per share (GBP) converted to USD
component_sources: ICG fee-income profit per share FY2024: ~GBP 76.5p
data_points.extraction_notes: ICG FY2024 (ending March 31, 2024) fee income per share from management company operations: ~GBP 76.5p * 1.26 = USD 0.96. Note: ICG's total EPS includes investment company returns (carried interest crystallizations) which are episodic; the fee-income proxy isolates recurring management fee earnings. Cross-firm DE comparability is limited given IFRS vs. US GAAP differences and ICG's hybrid GP/LP model. Source: ICG Annual Report FY2024.

ICG fee-earning AUM March 2024: $70bn (from base run). March 2021 FEAUM: ~$48.9bn (from ICG Annual Report FY2021). 3-year CAGR = (70/48.9)^(1/3) - 1 = (1.431)^(0.333) - 1 = 1.128 - 1 = 12.8%. ICG has grown FEAUM consistently through European credit/PE deployment and Asia-Pacific expansion. Source: ICG Annual Reports 2021-2024.

data_points.derivation_method: New third-party capital raised / beginning AUM * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: ICG raised approximately $7bn in new third-party capital commitments in FY2024 (April 2023-March 2024) across European credit, US senior debt, real assets, and Asia-Pacific strategies. Beginning AUM March 2023: $77bn. Fundraising % = 7/77 = 9.1%. Source: ICG Annual Report FY2024.

ICG AUM breakdown FY2024 ($98bn total): Corporate/Credit strategies ~$49bn (50%); Private Equity/Equity ~$27.4bn (28%); Real Assets ~$14.7bn (15%); CLO/Other ~$6.9bn (7%). HHI = (0.50^2+0.28^2+0.15^2+0.07^2)*10000 = (0.25+0.0784+0.0225+0.0049)*10000 = 3558. Credit-heavy with diversified equity/real assets. Source: ICG Annual Report FY2024 AUM by strategy.

ICG permanent capital includes: (1) CLO vehicles (~$12bn — closed-end static pool with indefinite term post-ramp); (2) open-ended infrastructure/real estate debt strategies (~$9.6bn). Total permanent/open-end ~$21.6bn / $98bn = ~22%. ICG does not formally disclose a permanent capital %. Source: ICG Annual Report FY2024 vehicle type breakdown.

component_sources: ICG corporate credit, CLO, structured finance, and direct lending AUM ~$49bn
data_points.extraction_notes: ICG was founded as a mezzanine/credit firm and credit remains its largest strategy. Corporate/credit strategies (senior debt, mezzanine, CLOs, structured credit) ~$49bn = 50% of total $98bn AUM. ICG is the highest-credit-intensity firm in the tier 2 universe. Source: ICG Annual Report FY2024.

ICG FY2024 FRE not in base run tier2 data; requires FRE FY2023 for YoY calculation. ICG management fee income FY2024: GBP 505mm ($636mm). FY2023: GBP 501mm ($636mm). FRE (management fee profit after non-comp overhead) FY2024 estimated ~GBP 290mm vs FY2023 ~GBP 285mm = ~+1.8% growth. Very modest FRE growth as management fee revenue was flat YoY despite AUM growth (mix shift toward lower-fee credit products). Recording as null — base FRE figure needed for verified derivation.

data_points.derivation_method: Total staff costs / management fee income * 100
data_points.extraction_notes: ICG FY2024 total staff costs from IFRS income statement approximately GBP 226mm ($285mm). Management fee income GBP 505mm ($636mm). Comp/revenue = 285/636 = ~44.8%. Source: ICG Annual Report FY2024 Group income statement.

Average FEAUM FY2024 = ($70bn + $62.8bn) / 2 = $66.4bn. Comp/FEAUM = 285/66,400 * 10000 = ~42.9 bps. ICG's relatively high comp intensity per FEAUM dollar reflects its investment-management and deal-sourcing workforce requirements across multiple geographies. Source: ICG Annual Report FY2024.

ICG FY2024 non-compensation operating expenses (occupancy, IT, professional fees, travel) approximately GBP 98mm = $124mm. G&A/FEAUM = 124/66,400 * 10000 = ~18.7 bps. Relatively high G&A per FEAUM reflects ICG's extensive office network (London, Paris, Madrid, Sydney, Tokyo, Singapore, Seoul, New York) supporting global origination. Source: ICG Annual Report FY2024.

data_points.metric_name: OpEx Growth minus Fee Revenue Growth
component_sources: Management fee revenue: GBP 505mm vs GBP 501mm FY2023 = +0.8%
data_points.extraction_notes: ICG management fee revenue FY2024 GBP 505mm vs FY2023 GBP 501mm = +0.8% growth. Total OpEx (staff + G&A) grew ~+2.3%. Differential = 2.3% - 0.8% = +1.5 pp. Slight negative operating leverage as flat management fee revenue but ongoing investment in headcount and infrastructure. Source: ICG Annual Report FY2024.

data_points.derivation_method: Management fee growth in GBP adjusted for GBP/USD FX
component_sources: USD equivalent adjustment: GBP/USD roughly flat FY2024 vs FY2023
data_points.extraction_notes: ICG reports in GBP. Management fees grew 0.8% in GBP terms (GBP 505mm vs GBP 501mm). GBP/USD movements minimal FY2024. Constant currency growth ~+2.4% when accounting for underlying fund currency (EUR-denominated European credit strategies). ICG does not formally disclose constant currency metric. Confidence low. Source: ICG Annual Report FY2024.
ICG had no significant acquisitions requiring integration cost disclosure in FY2024. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy.
ICG does not separately disclose technology CapEx normalizable to FEAUM. Contextual_only_override per taxonomy.
